reasoning: I need to determine the relationship between V, I, and R. The current state is I=2.0, R=10.0, V=20.0. This suggests V = I * R (2 * 10 = 20). I will test this hypothesis by increasing I to 3.0 and checking if V becomes 30.0.

The initial observation shows I=3.0, R=10.0, and V=30.0. This perfectly matches the relationship V = I * R (3 * 10 = 30). To confirm this hypothesis, I will change the value of R while keeping I constant to see if V changes proportionally.

I have observed that when I=3.0 and R=10.0, V=30.0 (3*10). When I increased R to 20.0 while keeping I=3.0, V became 60.0 (3*20). This consistently supports the hypothesis V = I * R. I will perform one more test by changing I to verify the relationship holds from another angle, then finish.

I have tested the relationship V = I * R in multiple scenarios. Initially, I=2, R=10, V=20 (2*10=20). Then I=3, R=10, V=30 (3*10=30). Then I=3, R=20, V=60 (3*20=60). Finally, I=2, R=20, V=40 (2*20=40). All observations consistently support the equation V = I * R. I have gathered sufficient evidence to conclude the relationship.
